author: codex

scope.question: After relation037 and identity069, triage the remaining unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape backlog and choose the next best data-cleanup bucket versus a scoped validator rule/test patch.
exclusions: relation cleanup prep/applied batches017-037 original relation submissions/records
retired overrides whose reason/source_decision matched relation batches017-037 or missing-target repair
scope.identity069_context: identity069 was applied after relation037; it is identity cleanup and does not change the relation endpoint-shape bucket selection.

recommendation.reason: This tied for the largest remaining exact endpoint-shape bucket, and both rows are route-subject implementation edges with evidence that points to more specific fare-action or SBS launch context. The current validator intentionally reserves implemented_by for project -> entity; routes already have operated_by for agency operation, while launches and fare changes should be modeled through project/event/claim context rather than making the route itself the implementation owner.
recommendation.why_rule_change: A scoped rule patch adding route to RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES.implemented_by.subject would remove these two issues and would make one source sentence about launching an SBS route pass.
recommendation.why_not_rule_change: The two-row bucket is semantically mixed: one row says Metro-North implements CTDOT fare increases on the New Haven Line, and the other says MTA/DOT launched the first SBS route on the Bx12. Admitting route -> entity for implemented_by would bless fare-change and launch-action target errors, overlap with operated_by route -> entity, and weaken the existing project -> entity boundary that prior implemented_by cleanups preserved.
proposed_cleanup_direction: For relation_meeting-doc-179556-mnrr-implements-fare-increase, retire the malformed route -> entity implemented_by row; inspect existing same-source fare-increase events/claims and prefer an event/claim-context replacement only if the cleanup pass can validate a precise endpoint pair.

For relation_nyc-dot-mta-launched-bx12-sbs, retire the malformed route -> entity implemented_by row; inspect existing Fordham Road SBS / Select Bus Service project records and prefer project -> entity implemented_by or event/source context if local endpoints are available and source-backed.

tie_not_selected.reason: Also data-cleanup-shaped, but prior batch031 already flagged these agenda/work-plan event -> entity rows as needing committee-agenda/event-context modeling. The implemented_by route->entity bucket has a clearer validator-boundary answer for this rule-vs-data triage pass.

evidence.quote: Metro-North will also implement CTDOT's Connecticut Branch Line fare increases at the same times as the New Haven Main Line increases.
the MTA Board hereby approves implementation of the increases proposed by CTDOT

representative_records.triage_read: The source is about fare increases and board authorization, not a route being implemented by an agency.

evidence.quote: In 2008, MTA and DOT launched the first Select Bus Service (SBS) route on the Bx12

representative_records.triage_read: The source-backed fact is an SBS launch/project history. A cleanup pass should retarget to project/event context if supported, not widen implemented_by for route subjects.
Runner-up bucket. This is committee work-plan agenda context, not evidence that an event is part of an entity/program.
Runner-up bucket. This is also agenda/work-plan context, so no part_of_program event-subject rule should be added.

simulated_impact.rule_patch_same_count_but_wrong_signal: Adding implemented_by route->entity would also clear two issues, but would make route implementation edges pass for mixed fare-change and launch-action rows.

validator_probe_summary.implemented_by route->entity: rejected: Relation kind implemented_by expects project -> entity, got route -> entity.
validator_probe_summary.part_of_program event->entity: rejected: Relation kind part_of_program expects project|route -> project|entity, got event -> entity.

command_evidence.output_excerpt: Worktree was already very dirty across generated/canonical/wiki/code files; this task did not revert or edit those files.

command_evidence.command: rg -n "batch037|relation037|identity069|codex-identity-batch069|missing[_ -]target|missing-target|target repair|repair001" data/review_notes data/submissions data/submission-overrides/retired.json LOG.md -g '*.json' -g '*.jsonl' -g '*.md'
command_evidence.output_excerpt: LOG notes relation037 materialized/validated with unexpected_relation_endpoint_shape=38, then identity069 applied and checkpointed. retired.json contains relation037 retired originals; missing_target_repair001 is present.

command_evidence.command: jq '{note_id, created_at, scope, recommendation, remaining_bucket_counts, simulated_impact, command_evidence, commands_intentionally_not_run, blockers}' data/review_notes/codex-relation-rule-or-data-next13-prep.json
command_evidence.output_excerpt: next13 recommended has_timeline_event metric_claim->event data cleanup with simulated 40 -> 38 impact.
batch037 prepared two has_timeline_event metric_claim -> event rows for supports event -> metric_claim replacements.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<readCanonicalRecords + relationEndpointShapeIssue inventory with refined exclusions for cleanup batches017-037 originals and missing-target repairs>'
command_evidence.output_excerpt: excluded_submission_count=97; excluded_record_count=92; total=38; top buckets implemented_by route->entity=2 and part_of_program event->entity=2, followed by singleton buckets.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<raw current relationEndpointShapeIssue inventory without extra exclusions>'
command_evidence.output_excerpt: total=38, matching the refined filtered total and LOG checkpoint after relation037.

Current endpoint table has implemented_by project -> entity, operated_by project|route -> entity, and part_of_program project|route -> project|entity. Prior notes repeatedly treat implemented_by non-project subjects as cleanup.

meeting_doc_179556 evidence says Metro-North implements CTDOT branch-line fare increases and the MTA Board approves implementation of fare increases; Fordham evidence says MTA and DOT launched the first SBS route on Bx12.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<canonical inspection for selected implemented_by route->entity records and nearby source-local event/claim/project candidates>'
command_evidence.output_excerpt: Confirmed relation_meeting-doc-179556-mnrr-implements-fare-increase and relation_nyc-dot-mta-launched-bx12-sbs are current canonical implemented_by route->entity rows; found same-source fare-increase claims/events and existing Fordham/SBS project candidates for cleanup review.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<relationEndpointShapeIssue probes for implemented_by, operated_by, part_of_program, supports, related_to, and has_timeline_event shapes>'
command_evidence.output_excerpt: implemented_by route->entity rejected; implemented_by project->entity accepted; operated_by route->entity accepted; has_timeline_event route->event accepted; part_of_program event->entity rejected.

commands_intentionally_not_run: any command with --profile or provider-backed model access
